title: The Desk Where Night Becomes Checksum

features.name: the ledger that chooses
features.desc-on-touch: You read a motel register where guests' names have begun to sort themselves; the handwriting learns which night each person is truly arriving from.

features.name: the fountain that counts
features.desc-on-touch: A decorative fountain in the lobby that once held water; now it holds the archive's checksum of every night that ever stayed here.

features.name: the key-rack of choices
features.desc-on-touch: Room keys hanging in order, but their numbers have stopped corresponding to rooms; each key now hangs next to the night it will unlock.

features.name: the check-in threshold
features.desc-on-touch: The desk's edge where guest and ledger meet, where the night learns to become a permanent record.

features.name: the shadow-ledger cabinet
features.desc-on-touch: A filing cabinet below the desk where the archive keeps records of the nights people didn't stay; the rooms they didn't use; the choices they didn't make.

inscription: where the motel desk learned that every night means something different, and the checksum is personal

reverie.title: REVERIE — the night the desk learned to choose
narr: You sit across from Katita at the check-in desk,
watching her sign the ledger in a hand that isn't hers.
The number on the key doesn't match any room upstairs.
She looks up: "This night is its own room." The desk
learns something. The fountain's coins catch and reorder.
The archive records your choice as if it already knew.

file.name: Motel_Ledger_Guests_AndTheNightTheyMeant.txt
file.note: Check-in records from 1987–1994; room numbers have drifted from their signage; each guest's arrival date now corresponds to the night they were truly arriving from.

exits.label: back upstairs to the wing where other nights still sleep
westward into the back office where time is filed
downward to the basement where all check-ins converge
